title: glaucium refractocarpum subsp. refractocarpum
intro: Glaucium refractocarpum subsp. refractocarpum, also known as the lesser sea spurge, is a plant species in the poppy family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce emesis and treat a variety of gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Glaucium refractocarpum subsp. refractocarpum include its use as a natural remedy for nausea, diarrhea, and other digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and seeds are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat these conditions.
